raw_output:
The document was classified as a financial_statement. Key signals included:  
• Prominent headings such as “Statement of Assets and Liabilities,” “Income Summary,” “Net Asset Value” and “Shareholder Distributions.”  
• Tabular line-item breakdowns of account holdings (e.g. “Total Assets,” “Total Liabilities,” “Net Assets”) and performance figures by date.  
• Absence of typical invoice elements (no “Invoice #,” “Payment Terms,” or “Amount Due”) or contract language (no “Parties,” “Term,” “Signature” sections) and no generic form fields to fill in.  

Together, the numeric tables and accounting-style terminology strongly point to a financial statement.
